hoses: Hydrant supply line to Engine 181 intake
Standpipe FDC Supply Line A: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose
Standpipe FDC Supply Line B: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose
Two interior standpipe attack lines: 150 ft of 2½-inch hose each
nozzles: Two 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzles, 265 GPM @ 50 PSI each

problems.title: Total Standpipe Attack Flow
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a standpipe operation. Two interior attack lines are operating at 265 GPM each. What total known standpipe attack flow should be used for the pump operation?
formulaBreakdown: Total standpipe flow = attack line A flow + attack line B flow.
problems.instructorExplanation: The standpipe operation has two known interior attack lines flowing 265 GPM each. Add both known standpipe attack flows for a total of 530 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count only one interior line. Both standpipe attack lines are flowing.

problems.title: Flow Per Standpipe FDC Supply Line
problems.question: Engine 181 supplies the Standpipe FDC with two equal supply lines. The known standpipe demand is 530 GPM. What flow should be used in each Standpipe FDC supply line?
problems.answerLabel: Flow Per Standpipe FDC Supply Line

answers.answerLabel: Flow Per Standpipe FDC Supply Line

formulaBreakdown: Flow per Standpipe FDC line = total standpipe flow ÷ number of supply lines.
Number of Standpipe FDC supply lines = 2.
problems.instructorExplanation: The two Standpipe FDC supply lines split the known standpipe demand equally. Each line carries 265 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each Standpipe FDC supply line using the full 530 GPM. Parallel supply lines split the flow.

problems.title: Standpipe FDC Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: Each Standpipe FDC supply line is 100 ft of 2 1/2-inch hose. The known standpipe demand is 530 GPM split equally between two supply lines. Use FL = C x (GPM/100)^2 x (length/100), with C = 2. What is the friction loss in each Standpipe FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C x (GPM / 100)^2 x hose length in hundreds.
Flow per Standpipe FDC line = 265 GPM.
problems.instructorExplanation: Each Standpipe FDC supply line carries 265 GPM. At 265 GPM through 100 ft of 2½-inch hose, friction loss rounds to 15 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the total 530 GPM in one supply line. Each of the two Standpipe FDC lines carries half the total flow.

problems.title: Standpipe Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying the Standpipe FDC with two equal 100 ft 2 1/2-inch supply lines. The known standpipe attack flow is 530 GPM; split that flow equally between the two Standpipe FDC supply lines. Each interior attack line is 150 ft of 2 1/2-inch hose flowing 265 GPM through a 1 1/8-inch smooth bore nozzle at 50 PSI. The fire is on the 7th floor. Use height = (floor number - 1) x 10 ft and elevation pressure = height x 0.5 PSI/ft. Add 25 PSI standpipe/FDC system loss. What pump discharge pressure should Engine 181 set for the standpipe operation?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
Friction loss = FDC supply line FL + interior attack line FL.
problems.instructorExplanation: Calculate one complete supply path from Engine 181 to the Standpipe FDC, through the standpipe system, and through the interior attack line. Add FDC supply line friction loss, interior hose friction loss, nozzle pressure, elevation, and standpipe/FDC loss.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add friction loss for both equal Standpipe FDC supply lines together. Parallel lines split the flow, so one complete supply path governs the pressure.

problems.title: Standpipe FDC Supply Line Count
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying the Standpipe FDC using two equal supply lines. How many supply lines are feeding the Standpipe FDC?
formulaBreakdown: Count the supply lines feeding the Standpipe FDC.
problems.instructorExplanation: The Standpipe FDC is supplied by two equal supply lines from Engine 181.
problems.explainMistake: Count only the supply lines feeding the Standpipe FDC.
